questions.prompt: What should happen when a user runs an interactive setup command without providing a source flag?
questions.description: This applies to afk setup and every setup area subcommand such as rules, skills, mcps, utils, and hooks.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Always ask which defaults source/origin to use. If no saved default exists, require the user to choose or enter one. If a saved default exists, preselect it so Enter continues with that source.

questions.prompt: When should AFK update the saved default source?
questions.description: Choosing a different source during an interactive setup can either be one-run-only or persisted.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Only the explicit default-source flag should persist a new default. Interactive choices and one-off source overrides should affect only the current setup run.

questions.prompt: Which flag name should be the user-facing command for changing the saved default source?
questions.description: The current code exposes --defaults-source. Your request said default source in singular terms, so this decides whether to rename, alias, or keep the existing flag.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Introduce --default-source <source> as the preferred flag. Keep --defaults-source <source> as a backwards-compatible alias unless you want a hard rename.

questions.prompt: How should --yes behave with source selection?
questions.description: --yes currently skips prompts, so source choice needs a deterministic rule.
questions.recommendedAnswer: --yes should not prompt. It should use an explicit one-run source if provided, otherwise the saved default if present, otherwise the built-in AFK source.
